# 既存 DRY 違反の一掃 — 横断・既存重複を正典へ集約する

## Tasks (10/13 resolved)

### S1 — D1: Track-ID validation — delegate to domain canonical

> Remove the standalone validate_track_id / slug-validation copies from the 3 usecase modules (T001) and the 2 CLI sites (T002). Each removal replaces the local function with a direct call to domain::TrackId::try_new, mapping the domain error to the module-local error type.
> These two tasks are independent at the source-file level and can be worked in parallel, but each must produce its own commit (CN-04).

- [x] **T001**: [D1-usecase] Remove standalone validate_track_id copies from usecase: delete the private validate_track_id functions and associated inline test blocks in catalogue_impl_signals/mod.rs, type_signals/interactor.rs, and baseline_capture/mod.rs; replace every call site with TrackId::try_new from domain, mapping the DomainError to the module-local error variant. (`d044848b92dc9c05264b4651a70517c2f325ab9b`)
- [x] **T002**: [D1-cli] Remove standalone validate_track_id / validate_track_id_str copies from CLI layer: delete validate_track_id_str_local in apps/cli-composition/src/verify.rs and validate_track_id_str in apps/cli/src/commands/track/validate.rs; replace each call site with TrackId::try_new (domain) delegation. Verify cargo make ci passes. (`66c7e2c24e0ac86690fdc9d3e8bf23eda942d4b0`)

### S2 — D2: Empty/whitespace invariant — delegate to NonEmptyString

> Replace inline is_empty / trim().is_empty guards across the domain layer with NonEmptyString::try_new delegation. Split into three commits by file group to stay under 500 lines (CN-04):
> T003: ids.rs (ReviewGroupName::try_new guard).
> T004: plan.rs + spec.rs (4+ guards across two files).
> T005: impl_plan.rs + review_v2/types.rs (3 guards).
> Internal String field types are NOT changed (D2 / OQ-3).

- [x] **T003**: [D2-ids] Replace inline is_empty/trim().is_empty guards in domain ids.rs with NonEmptyString::try_new delegation: specifically the ReviewGroupName::try_new guard at ids.rs line 214-217 (the NonEmptyString::try_new at 178-181 is already the canonical form and stays). Extract a shared private fn validate_trimmed_non_empty if needed to avoid a 1-line duplication inside the same file. (`8f2999655b579c848a827ef65f00736f4474d68e`)
- [x] **T004**: [D2-plan-spec] Replace inline trim().is_empty guards in domain plan.rs (lines 24-30 for id and title) and spec.rs (lines 52-53, 149-151, 212-216 for text/title/version fields) with NonEmptyString::try_new delegation at the constructor call sites. Internal String storage is preserved (OQ-3). Verify cargo make ci passes. (`da90fcf73d1df586095661317cb95ae52bde721c`)
- [x] **T005**: [D2-impl-plan-review] Replace inline trim().is_empty guards in domain impl_plan.rs (line 196-198 for task description) and review_v2/types.rs (lines 24, 72, 233 for ReviewGroupName/ReviewerFinding message fields — the is_empty guards, NOT the is_empty query methods on lines 128-129 and 193) with NonEmptyString::try_new delegation. Verify cargo make ci passes. (`ad56a16f3979ceba739ba7dbd793f8827499634d`)

### S3 — D3: Subprocess / SHA-256 helpers — extract to shared infrastructure module

> T006 extracts spawn_codex, drain_pipe, tee_stderr_to_file, codex_bin, and the unified runtime_path(base_dir,...) builder into the existing shared infrastructure::codex_common module, making both codex_reviewer.rs and codex_dry_checker.rs call the shared versions. The run_codex_child timeout-poll loop is NOT shared — it stays per-module (CN-03: return types, output parsing, and kill strategy differ between the two adapters). To eliminate the pre-existing cross-layer duplicate in the apps/cli planner, tee_stderr_to_file is exposed pub and re-exported via apps/cli-composition so the planner reuses it through cli_composition (single source; apps/cli stays cli_composition-only; the I/O helper stays in infrastructure per CN-02).
> T007 replaces the inline format!("{hash_bytes:x}") at codex_dry_checker.rs:261 with a call to the existing infrastructure::dry_check::corpus::sha256_hex.
> NOTE — 4-source git-diff union (deferred): The review_v2::diff_getter (GitDiffGetter -> Vec<FilePath>, BTreeSet) and dry_check::diff_getter (GitDryCheckDiffGetter -> Vec<DiffFileHunks>, BTreeMap) implement different ports with fundamentally different output types and line-level vs file-level semantics. Source verification confirms they are port-specific parallel structures (analogous to the lock-acquisition pattern per D3/OQ-2) and are NOT cleanly extractable without creating cross-port coupling. This sub-item is deferred; no task is authored.

- [x] **T006**: [D3-subprocess] Extract the byte-for-byte duplicated Codex subprocess helpers from the two infrastructure Codex adapters (codex_reviewer.rs, codex_dry_checker.rs) into the existing shared module infrastructure::codex_common: spawn_codex, drain_pipe, tee_stderr_to_file, codex_bin, and the unified runtime_path(base_dir, prefix, ext) builder (merging runtime_path / prepare_runtime_path) become shared helpers; both adapters call the shared versions. The run_codex_child timeout-poll loop stays per-module (CN-03: return types, output parsing, and kill strategy differ between reviewer and dry-checker — sharing it would force a closure-based API that obscures these distinctions). To eliminate the pre-existing cross-layer duplicate of tee_stderr_to_file in the apps/cli planner (plan/codex_local.rs), tee_stderr_to_file is exposed pub from codex_common, re-exported from apps/cli-composition (pub use), and the planner reuses it via cli_composition::tee_stderr_to_file — single source, with apps/cli still depending only on cli_composition (architecture-rules.json) and the I/O helper staying in infrastructure (CN-02). The remaining shared helpers stay pub(crate). Respects CN-02/CN-03. (`1a001f56df5d41e8ce5664b59b462deb71378372`)
- [x] **T007**: [D3-sha256] Replace the inline format!("{hash_bytes:x}") at codex_dry_checker.rs line 261 with a call to the existing infrastructure::dry_check::corpus::sha256_hex, eliminating the semantic dup with corpus fingerprinting. Verify cargo make ci passes. (`3772ff2241292e8b9a2673e3686734b0c4d69f32`)

### S4 — D4: Test helpers — DEFERRED (OS-06 / ADR D4 note)

> DEFERRED per spec OS-06 and ADR D4 note: the test-helper sub-cluster (CwdGuard, init_git_repo, usecase stub bindings) is excluded from this track. The DRY gate cannot register ADR-D4-mandated per-boundary/cross-crate test-helper duplicates as accepted (no accept-list, no layer-aware judge context); proceeding would leave the gate permanently blocked on intentional residual copies that the ADR explicitly permits. Tasks T008/T009/T010 are retained in impl-plan.json for traceability with status 'skipped'. Re-engage when the gate capability constraint is resolved (see OS-06 Reassess-When conditions in the ADR D4 note).
> T008 (skipped): CwdGuard / CurrentDirGuard consolidation.
> T009 (skipped): init_git_repo / init_git_repo_on_track_branch consolidation.
> T010 (skipped): stub_binding / StubLayerBindings / NoLayersBindings consolidation.

- [-] **T008**: [D4-test-helpers-cwd] DEFERRED — per ADR D4 note and spec OS-06: the DRY gate cannot record ADR-D4-mandated per-boundary cross-crate test-helper duplicates as accepted; deferred until accept-list or layer-aware judge context is available. Original scope: Consolidate CwdGuard / CurrentDirGuard RAII definitions to one definition per test-compilation boundary — never crossing crate boundaries via dev-visible API. (a) libs/infrastructure: add a single #[cfg(test)] pub(crate) CurrentDirGuard in a shared test_support submodule and replace the independent copies in libs/infrastructure/src/git_cli/mod.rs and libs/infrastructure/src/dry_check/diff_getter.rs with imports of the shared definition. (b) apps/cli-composition: the shared canonical already exists as process_guards::CwdGuard in apps/cli-composition/src/review_v2/helpers.rs (#[cfg(test)]); migrate the local inline CwdGuard in apps/cli-composition/src/review_v2/commit_hash.rs (#[cfg(test)] mod tests) to use that shared canonical instead of its own copy — no other independent CwdGuard struct definition exists in apps/cli-composition. (c) apps/cli — src unit-test boundary only: both CurrentDirGuard copies in apps/cli/src/commands/git.rs (#[cfg(test)] mod tests) and apps/cli/src/commands/review/tests.rs (#[cfg(test)] mod tests) are exclusively within the src unit-test compilation boundary; no CwdGuard usage exists anywhere in apps/cli/tests/; consolidate to a single #[cfg(test)] pub(crate) definition (e.g. a test_support module in apps/cli/src/) and update both call sites — the feasible minimum for apps/cli is one definition total. Do NOT create a workspace dev-dependency, a public test-support crate, or any public/feature-gated cross-crate API. References IN-04/OS-06. Verify cargo make ci passes.
- [-] **T009**: [D4-test-helpers-git-init] DEFERRED — per ADR D4 note and spec OS-06: the DRY gate cannot record ADR-D4-mandated per-boundary cross-crate test-helper duplicates as accepted; deferred until accept-list or layer-aware judge context is available. Original scope: Consolidate init_git_repo / init_git_repo_on_track_branch test helpers to one definition per test-compilation boundary — never crossing crate boundaries via dev-visible API. (a) libs/infrastructure: both helpers already live in a single file (libs/infrastructure/src/track/render_tests.rs) with no cross-file duplication within the crate; no copies to remove, but canonicalise git flags (commit.gpgsign=false, --no-gpg-sign) in that file's definitions. (b) apps/cli-composition — src unit-test boundary: two independent copies exist — init_git_repo in apps/cli-composition/src/review_v2/commit_hash.rs (#[cfg(test)] mod tests) and init_git_repo in apps/cli-composition/src/track/mod.rs (#[cfg(test)] block); both are in the same src unit-test compilation boundary; consolidate to a single #[cfg(test)] pub(crate) definition in a shared test_support module and update both call sites. (c) apps/cli — two separate test-compilation boundaries: (c1) src unit-test boundary: three independent copies of init_git_repo_on_track_branch in apps/cli/src/commands/track/tddd/baseline.rs, apps/cli/src/commands/track/tddd/signals.rs, and apps/cli/src/commands/track/tddd/catalogue_spec_signals.rs (all #[cfg(test)] blocks); consolidate to a single #[cfg(test)] pub(crate) definition in a shared test_support module in apps/cli/src/ and update the three call sites. (c2) integration-test boundary: apps/cli/tests/transition_integration.rs defines init_git_repo_on_track_branch at file level — this is the only definition in this boundary and is NOT a redundant copy; retain in place or extract to tests/common/mod.rs if other files in apps/cli/tests/ also use it; either way this is a legitimate per-boundary definition. The per-boundary feasible minimum for apps/cli is one in src + one in tests/: this is NOT a DRY violation, it is the unavoidable consequence of the Rust test-compilation model. apps/cli is explicitly permitted to have up to two init_git_repo definitions (one per boundary). Canonicalise git flags in all retained definitions. Do NOT create a workspace dev-dependency, a public test-support crate, or any public/feature-gated cross-crate API. References IN-04/OS-06. Verify cargo make ci passes.
- [-] **T010**: [D4-test-helpers-stubs] DEFERRED — per ADR D4 note and spec OS-06: the DRY gate cannot record ADR-D4-mandated per-boundary cross-crate test-helper duplicates as accepted; deferred until accept-list or layer-aware judge context is available. ### S5 — D4: Constants — consolidate to single definitions per architectural-layer boundary

> T011: POLL_INTERVAL (Duration::from_millis(50)) — consolidate ALL redundant within-boundary occurrences (both const definitions and inline literals) into a single const per layer-reachability boundary. The implementer determines exact placement. Same-literal occurrences across a forbidden layer boundary are coincidental constants retained as-is (per AC-05). Do NOT introduce cross-layer production dependencies.
> T012: "tmp/reviewer-runtime" path — consolidate ALL redundant within-boundary occurrences (both const definitions and inline literals) into a single const per layer-reachability boundary. This literal spans infrastructure / usecase / cli-composition / cli layers, making exact placement and per-boundary grouping an implementation-time decision. The implementer determines placement respecting architecture-rules.json. Same-literal occurrences across a forbidden layer boundary are coincidental constants retained as-is (per AC-05). Do NOT introduce cross-layer production dependencies.
> Each task produces its own commit.
